Explanation: This report addresses automated peak detection in high-variance chromatographic data and proposes a preprocessing methodology combining AsLS smoothing, negative value clipping, and inter-peak valley clustering. Strengths include clear problem articulation, well-designed visualizations (Figures 1-2 effectively contrast noisy vs. clean runs), and practical code implementation. However, the report has notable limitations: (1) Methodology section is incomplete with truncated text describing key parameters; (2) limited quantitative validation—no comparative metrics between proposed and traditional approaches; (3) Results present only two illustrative cases without statistical generalization; (4) Scientific rigor is compromised by lack of experimental design clarity and no reported false-positive/false-negative rates; (5) the code appendix, while comprehensive, suggests ad-hoc implementation rather than validated methodology. The inter-peak valley clustering concept is moderately novel but insufficiently differentiated from existing approaches. Writing quality is generally acceptable but contains formatting issues and incomplete sentences in the Methodology section.
